name: UP Community Fund
description_short: UP Community Fund provides growth capital to small business owners with $300K-$5M revenue, emphasizing impact-focused lending to women entrepreneurs and communities of color.
description_long:
UP Community Fund is a mission-driven lender focused on providing growth capital to small business owners who lack access to traditional financing. The organization explicitly targets entrepreneurs seeking to create greater economic and societal impact within their communities, positioning itself as more than a transactional lender.

The company offers customized funding solutions tailored to individual business needs, with their primary lending window serving businesses with annual revenue between $300,000 and $5 million. Their approach includes three core components: understanding the business deeply, customizing the funding solution, and providing ongoing expertise beyond the initial loan transaction. As of their 2020 Impact Report, UP Community Fund has funded $9.2 million across their portfolio, with an average loan size of $576,000.

What distinguishes UP Community Fund is their explicit commitment to impact-focused lending and diversity metrics. The organization highlights that 40% of their funded entrepreneurs are women entrepreneurs of color, suggesting deliberate underserved-market focus. They also reference "The UP Village," indicating a community support component beyond capital provision. This emphasis on community and ongoing support differentiates them from conventional small business lenders focused purely on loan economics.

Limitations worth noting: the website provides minimal detail on loan terms, interest rates, repayment periods, or specific eligibility criteria beyond revenue thresholds. The $576K average loan size suggests this is not micro-lending, and the $300K revenue minimum excludes very early-stage or pre-revenue businesses. Geographic service area is not specified. The 2020 Impact Report reference suggests data may not be current, and their website lacks details on application timelines, approval rates, or recent lending activity.
pros: Explicitly serves women entrepreneurs and entrepreneurs of color (40% of portfolio)
Provides customized funding solutions rather than one-size-fits-all products
Offers ongoing expertise and support beyond capital deployment
Serves underserved small business market with $300K-$5M revenue sweet spot
Impact-focused lending aligned with community economic development
Average $576K loan size enables meaningful business growth capital
Transparent about portfolio metrics and publishes impact reports
cons: Website lacks specific information on interest rates, terms, or APR
No clear explanation of detailed eligibility criteria beyond revenue range
Most recent public impact data is from 2020; current lending activity unclear
Minimum $300K revenue threshold excludes very early-stage businesses and startups
best_for: Women entrepreneurs and entrepreneurs of color seeking growth capital with values-aligned lenders
Small business owners with $300K-$5M revenue needing customized funding solutions
Impact-focused businesses prioritizing community and social outcomes alongside profitability
Entrepreneurs seeking ongoing mentorship and expertise alongside capital
diagnosis: UP Community Fund is best for small business owners with $300K-$5M revenue—particularly women entrepreneurs and entrepreneurs of color—who seek capital alongside ongoing business support and want to work with a lender aligned with their community impact values. The main caveat is the lack of transparent pricing information on rates and terms, making it essential to contact them directly for specific loan cost comparisons.
services: Growth capital/business loans for small business owners
Customized funding solutions tailored to individual business needs
Ongoing expertise and business support post-funding
Impact-focused lending to underserved entrepreneur communities
Portfolio support through the UP Village community platform
Impact reporting and transparent portfolio metrics tracking
